seoTitle: Mount Cotton vs Mount Crosby — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Cotton and Mount Crosby. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Cotton vs Mount Crosby: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Cotton and Mount Crosby.
heading: Mount Cotton vs Mount Crosby — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Cotton, Queensland and Mount Crosby, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Crosby has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount Cotton (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Cotton or Mount Crosby safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Cotton has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Mount Crosby scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Cotton scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Crosby scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Cotton or Mount Crosby?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Crosby has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
